spec_worktree: none (OKF-only work on main checkout; domain-discovery spec worktree was cleaned up after #12-14)
objective: Ratify the hotspots and Context Map corrections accumulated across issues #12-15 event-storming records into one canonical Context Map conclusion, closing parent spec issue #1.
invariants: Every hotspot is either resolved with an explicit decision or deferred to a named later issue (glossary / ADR / tactical design).
Context Map corrections from issue #13 (Tool Lab GitHub access boundary; Orchestration grade-check skip stale-use risk) are confirmed or recorded as explicit decisions.
No runtime RiskLevel is inferred from GitHub Project Risk; they are different models.
This record confirms, defers, or records; it does not reopen resolved decisions or invent new ones.
Ratification is R1 human-required; no agent may infer, manufacture, or replay the product-owner decision.
decisions: The Context Map conclusion record lives at my-docs/okf/context-map/conclusion.md as a distinct canonical artifact class from event-storming records (product-owner choice: option B).
17 resolved hotspots (9 from #12, 8 from #13) are confirmed as-is, carried verbatim from the accepted records.

2 Context Map corrections from #13 are confirmed: Tool Lab public-unauthenticated GitHub access boundary with private/credential access behind Integration ACL; and Orchestration grade-check skip with the stale-use risk recorded.

7 deferred hotspots are routed to named later issues: #1 Runtime RiskLevel -> issue #3 + A&S tactical; #2 Tool Lab->Knowledge contract name/schema -> issue #2; #3 Minimum provenance schema -> issue #2 + tactical; #4 'Meaningfully changed' threshold -> issue #2 + tactical; #5 IndexState canonical names -> issue #2; #6 Direct Published Language vs Integration ACL route -> issue #3 / Context Map tactical; #7 OKF/Git partial-failure recovery -> later tactical design / KnowledgeWriter recovery ADR under #3 scope.
Hotspot #7 (OKF/Git recovery) appears in both #14 and #15 tables; it is recorded once and deferred so no hotspot is left unclassified, satisfying the conclusion invariant.
The issue #13 'repository Tool vs Skill' hotspot resolution is to defer re-testing SkillVersion to issue #2; this deferral is the explicit resolution and is confirmed here as resolved.
This is pure OKF ratification work: it belongs on the main checkout (or a short-lived feature branch off main), not a spec worktree, because main owns durable OKF per ADR-0001.
implementation_state: Context Map conclusion record ratified by the product owner on 2026-07-14 and moved to Prod. The record at my-docs/okf/context-map/conclusion.md aggregates 31 hotspots across the four event-storming records, confirms 17 resolved, confirms 2 Context Map corrections, and defers 7 unresolved to issues #2, #3, or later tactical design. Branch feature/16-ratify-context-map was merged into main and pushed. Parent spec issue #1 may move to Done because both exit criteria are satisfied. No code changed; this is pure OKF ratification.

open_loops: Deferred hotspot ownership: issues #2 and #3 must consume the 7 deferred hotspots; issue #2 owns hotspots #2-#5 (Tool Lab->Knowledge contract name/schema, minimum provenance schema, 'meaningfully changed' threshold, IndexState canonical names), issue #3 owns #1 (runtime RiskLevel) and #6 (direct Published Language vs Integration ACL route) and the KnowledgeWriter recovery ADR for #7.

A KnowledgeUpdateRequested (kur-) JSON record was not created for #16 because the schema requires a pull_request reference and #16 is OKF-only on a feature branch with no PR; if the operating model later requires it, create it once a PR or equivalent provenance reference exists.
The Context Map is Phase-0-only; the Integration ACL route decision (deferred hotspot #6) and deeper relationships are ratified later under issue #3.
next_action: Issue #1 and #16 are Done. Per Faz 0 ordering, the next issues in scope are ArifMolon/yula#2 (Freeze Phase 0 Ubiquitous Language) and then #3 (Ratify Phase 0 architecture ADRs); alternatively #4 (executable monorepo foundation) may come first. The product owner must explicitly place the next issue in scope.
